result:
Data cleaning completed successfully. The cleaned dataset has been saved to data/output/cleaned_data.csv with 16,111 observations and 7 variables:

1. NSID - cohort member identifier (original ID variable from source data)
2. eth - harmonized ethnicity variable (time-invariant, prioritizing earliest valid response)
3. eth14 - ethnicity at age 14 (Wave 1)
4. eth15 - ethnicity at age 15 (Wave 2)
5. eth17 - ethnicity at age 17 (Wave 4)
6. eth25 - ethnicity at age 25 (Wave 8)
7. eth32 - ethnicity at age 32 (Wave 9)

Key cleaning operations performed:
- Loaded 5 tab-delimited files using readr::read_delim()
- Merged datasets using full_join by NSID
- Converted wave-specific missing codes to standard codes (-9=Refusal, -8=Don't know/insufficient, -1=Not applicable, -3=Not asked)
- Replaced NA values with -3 (not asked)
- Created harmonized ethnicity variable prioritizing earliest valid response
- Created age-specific ethnicity variables with standardized naming convention
- Applied factor labels for all valid categories and missing value codes
- Output only the ID and derived variables to the final CSV
